body_preview:
Hi Meg,

Before the week gets away from me — Yuki mentioned she and Kenji are free Saturday the 21st for dinner. I thought we could finally try that izakaya near Okazaki we've been circling for months. Low-key, nothing elaborate.

That said, I know you me
subject: Hi Meg

body_preview:
Ethan,

Loading starts Friday evening. Toshio wants the chamber sealed by Saturday at dawn so we can light at first light — he's particular about that transition. I'll be at the kiln through the weekend; the wood-ash glaze pieces from last month's pulling
subject: Re: Ethan

body_preview:
Dear Mom,

The anagama firing finished last Tuesday — three days of stoking, and the wood-ash settled exactly where I'd hoped on a tea bowl I've been shaping since February. I'm sending it to you. It should arrive Thursday via EMS; handle it carefully unt
subject: Dear Mom

body_preview:
Dear Margaret,

It arrived this morning. I opened it at the kitchen table with your father looking over my shoulder and we both went quiet. That amber along the rim catches the light just as you described. I've put it on the windowsill for now, though you
subject: Re: Dear Margaret

body_preview:
Dear Mom,

The windowsill is exactly right — Toshio-sensei would actually approve. He says a piece finds its place eventually, and I believe him.

I'm sleeping fine. The firing schedule is demanding, but it's the concentrated kind of tired I find satisfyi
subject: Re: Dear Mom

body_preview:
Dear Margaret,

Your mother found the Shibui Gallery announcement in her inbox this morning and has talked of little else since. A September opening — is there enough time for us to book flights and arrange something decent nearby? We haven't been back to
subject: Dear Margaret

body_preview:
Dad,

The opening is Saturday the 13th of September, six to nine in the evening. The show runs through the 28th, so any weekend in that window works. I have fourteen pieces in the cone 10 reduction series — the largest took three months on and off the whe
subject: Re: Dad

body_preview:
Dear Margaret,

Done — flights booked for the 12th, returning the 16th. Carol insisted we land a full day early and she was right. We'll look into the inn on Marutamachi.

I have to ask: cone 10 reduction. I know what each word means individually. Is ther
subject: Re: Dear Margaret

body_preview:
Dad,

Cone 10 is roughly 1300 degrees Celsius. Reduction means the kiln is starved of oxygen during the firing, which draws colour from the clay body itself — iron in the clay shifts from rust to grey-blue to near-black depending on where each piece sits
subject: Re: Dad

body_preview:
Daniel,

Mum mentioned she's considering Kyoto sometime in September. Before she commits to anything, I need to flag that I'm scheduled for a week-long anagama firing the third week — likely the 15th through the 22nd. That stretch is not manageable for ho
subject: Daniel

body_preview:
Margaret,

Noted on the firing window — I'll have a word with her. She actually mentioned October already as a loose preference, so this may resolve itself without diplomacy. The open kiln event sounds like precisely the kind of thing she'd photograph fro
subject: Re: Margaret

body_preview:
Saito-sensei,

The October anagama firing at Shigaraki — I wanted to ask whether there is still shelf space available. I have approximately 40 pieces ready: predominantly yunomi and a few larger hachi forms that have been drying slowly since August.

I ha
subject: Saito-sensei

body_preview:
Margaret-san,

There is room. Loading begins on the 14th, and I have reserved the middle-right section of the main chamber for you — the ash deposit runs heavy there by the end of day three. Given what you are testing, that placement will tell you more th
subject: Re: Margaret-san

body_preview:
Saito-sensei,

Thank you. The middle-right section suits the work well — the hachi forms especially need the surface variation that placement will provide.

The recipe: Colemanite 15, Silica 25, Custer Feldspar 30, Whiting 20, unwashed cedar ash 10. I hav
subject: Re: Saito-sensei

body_preview:
Mika,

Saito-sensei mentioned he is planning a November anagama firing — a three-day stoking cycle beginning around the 14th. He has space for additional work and suggested I reach out to you before he closes the load list.

I am planning to fire a series
subject: Mika

body_preview:
Margaret,

Thank you for thinking of me — I have been waiting for an anagama opportunity since the spring firing filled before I could join.

I currently have twenty-two pieces in progress: yunomi and small vases thrown in a local Shigaraki clay body. I h
subject: Re: Margaret

body_preview:
Mika,

The firing runs full reduction from cone 6 onward — Saito-sensei does not moderate in the back half. Your Shigaraki pieces should respond well to that; the iron will be active.

I am placing my bowls on the middle shelves, set back roughly one mete
subject: Re: Mika

body_preview:
Margaret,

The mouth placement is exactly what I was hoping for. The yunomi especially will benefit from heavier ash deposit, and I am willing to accept some unpredictability in exchange for the surface quality that position tends to produce.

I will have
subject: Re: Margaret

body_preview:
Hi Meg,

Before the week gets away from me — Yuki mentioned she and Kenji are free Saturday the 21st for dinner. I thought we could finally try that izakaya near Okazaki we've been circling for months. Low-key, nothing elaborate.

That said, I know you me
